searchIndex.title: Research priority: structured objective data production for evaluations
searchIndex.body: gad-61 research priority: structured objective data production for evaluations the primary goal of the eval framework is no longer "score runs" — it's "produce structured data that yields insights." a score is just one kind of derived data; the framework should also produce structured human review rubrics (dimensions instead of single numbers), automated gate checks (playwright tests instead of human inspection for things automatable), derived metrics (tool mix, commit rhyth

searchIndex.title: Trace event output field capped at 4 KB with head/tail truncation
searchIndex.body: gad-60 trace event output field capped at 4 kb with head/tail truncation individual event `outputs` fields in the jsonl stream are capped at 4096 bytes. when raw output exceeds the cap, it's split into 2 kb of head + 2 kb of tail joined by a "... [truncated n bytes] ..." marker, and the event is stamped with `truncated: true`. full untruncated output remains in claude code's session.jsonl which is accessible on disk for any run that needs deeper inspection. the 4 kb ca

searchIndex.title: Hook handler lives in the GAD repo, referenced by absolute path from user settings
searchIndex.body: gad-59 hook handler lives in the gad repo, referenced by absolute path from user settings the hook handler script lives at vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs (or equivalent in the distributed gad package). `gad install hooks` writes an absolute path reference into the user's ~/.claude/settings.json pointing at the installed gad binary. this gives us one canonical handler per framework commit, zero per-workspace drift, updates via `gad update`. `gad uninstall hooks` is the

searchIndex.title: Skill invocation marker: hybrid file + hook-emitted event
searchIndex.body: gad-58 skill invocation marker: hybrid file + hook-emitted event the agent writes a single plain-text file at .planning/.trace-active-skill containing just the active skill id. that's the entire agent-side burden — one file write per skill start. the hook handler reads this file on every pretooluse event to attribute subsequent tool uses to the active skill (adds trigger_skill field on each event) and detects content changes to emit a discrete skill_invocation

searchIndex.title: Remotion parameters: 30s max, cinematic passthrough, stoppable, /videos route + inline embeds
searchIndex.body: gad-57 remotion parameters: 30s max, cinematic passthrough, stoppable, /videos route + inline embeds phase 26 parameters pinned: every composition is max 30 seconds, rendered as a cinematic passthrough (linear timeline, no branching), but the player must expose play/pause so viewers can stop mid-scroll. videos live in two places: (1) inline "watch this dissection" embeds on relevant per-run pages — e.g. the v8 failure dissection shows up on /runs/escape-the-dungeon/v8, and (2) a dedicated /videos

searchIndex.title: Lineage attribution: GSD → RepoPlanner → GAD
searchIndex.body: gad-56 lineage attribution: gsd → repoplanner → gad gad's lineage is acknowledged explicitly on the public site. get shit done (gsd-build, https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done) is the upstream inspiration — small planning loops, visible state, executable specs. repoplanner (b2gdevs, https://repo-planner.vercel.app/) was an earlier attempt by the same author to formalize the "ralph wiggum loop" (continuous agent work maintained by in-repo docu

searchIndex.title: Multi-agent tracing is a conversion problem, not a reimplementation problem
searchIndex.body: gad-55 multi-agent tracing is a conversion problem, not a reimplementation problem if we ever want to evaluate codex, aider, or other hook-capable agents, the path is: their native session format → a converter script that emits gad trace schema v4 → the same /runs/[project]/[version] page renders it. no per-agent site code. codex's output format (running/ran prefixes with output blocks under ` └ `) is parseable but stream-based — would need a converter that tails the session and

searchIndex.title: Eval backfill policy: freeze old scores, stamp new runs with framework version
searchIndex.body: gad-54 eval backfill policy: freeze old scores, stamp new runs with framework version when the framework changes (new skill, new subagent, rewritten command), old eval scores are never recomputed. they're historical records, frozen at the framework commit they ran against. new runs against the updated framework get stamped with the new framework commit and become the "current" data point for cross-version comparisons. this preserves the archaeology — a reader can see what a v5 esca

searchIndex.title: Trace schema v4 requires hook-aware agents — other coding agents are explicitly unsupported for tracing
searchIndex.body: gad-53 trace schema v4 requires hook-aware agents — other coding agents are explicitly unsupported for tracing phase 25's trace schema v4 captures every tool_use, skill invocation with trigger context, and subagent spawn with inputs/outputs. the only reliable way to capture this data is from within the coding agent's runtime via hooks — claude code's pretooluse/posttooluse hooks, aider's python callbacks, continue.dev's extension api. agents without hook runtimes (cursor's closed-source editor, basic codex

searchIndex.title: Remotion is how we ship video explainers — player, not compiler
searchIndex.body: gad-52 remotion is how we ship video explainers — player, not compiler remotion's @remotion/player is a react component that renders video compositions live in the browser without requiring mp4 export. free for teams ≤3 and reuses existing react components (siteheader, score cards, svg charts) as video frames. no separate asset pipeline — video is composable react. phase 26 scopes what videos we want (gad loop walkthrough, v8 failure dissection with tracing overlay,

searchIndex.title: Framework is versioned on branches so evals can pin + re-run across versions
searchIndex.body: gad-51 framework is versioned on branches so evals can pin + re-run across versions main = current. frozen snapshots live on version/v1.1, version/v1.2, etc. when we ship a framework change that could affect eval scores (new skill, changed command, different subagent wiring), we branch, tag, and update a framework_version constant used by `gad version`. every trace.json stamps the commit it ran against so we can distinguish "the agent did better" from "the framework changed." re-

searchIndex.title: Trace schema v4: every tool use, skill invocation, and subagent spawn must be captured
searchIndex.body: gad-50 trace schema v4: every tool use, skill invocation, and subagent spawn must be captured today's trace.json has a bare numeric skill_accuracy field for most runs (e.g. escape-the-dungeon v8 = 0.17 with no breakdown of which of the 6 expected triggers fired vs missed). this means we can't interpret scores across workflows. trace schema v4 will capture: (1) every tool_use with timestamp + name + outcome; (2) every skill invocation with trigger context (user phrase, auto-detected, explic

searchIndex.title: Bare and Emergent bootstrap skill set is a pinned invariant: create-skill + find-sprites
searchIndex.body: gad-49 bare and emergent bootstrap skill set is a pinned invariant: create-skill + find-sprites any bare or emergent greenfield eval template must include skills/create-skill.md and skills/find-sprites.md in its template/skills/ directory before the eval runs. these two are the minimum bootstrap methodology an agent needs to author its own workflow (create-skill) and source coherent visual assets (find-sprites). emergent projects may inherit additional skills from previous runs, but the two-

searchIndex.title: GAD framework diagnosis (freedom hypothesis) is deferred — need more eval data first
searchIndex.body: gad-48 gad framework diagnosis (freedom hypothesis) is deferred — need more eval data first the round 3 findings showing bare consistently beats gad on creative implementation are enough to treat as a real pattern but not yet enough to commit to structural changes (gad-lite, pivoting away from creative evals, etc.). before making architectural decisions about gad itself, we need: (1) round 4 greenfield with v4 (pressure-oriented) requirements to see if the pattern persists; (2) brownfiel

searchIndex.title: Portfolio must host all experiment documentation before further experimentation
searchIndex.body: gad-47 portfolio must host all experiment documentation before further experimentation user wants to view all experiment docs on apps/portfolio: links to code, game builds, scores, process, workflows, graphs, explanations of what's being tested. the portfolio acts as the public showcase of the experiment. before running any more eval rounds, the portfolio must contain: (1) hub page explaining the experiment, the conditions (gad/bare/emergent), and the freedom hypothesis; (2) per-rou

searchIndex.title: Skills evolution flow: greenfield emergent → brownfield emergent → greenfield (installed back)
searchIndex.body: gad-46 skills evolution flow: greenfield emergent → brownfield emergent → greenfield (installed back) the experimental model for emergent workflows: (1) greenfield emergent creates initial skills from scratch (only has create_skill skill); (2) brownfield emergent inherits those skills + creates new ones as it extends existing code; (3) when proven, those skills can be "installed back" into the next greenfield emergent run as its inheritance. this creates a feedback loop where ai-decided workflows

searchIndex.title: Emergent AGENTS.md stops mandating specific artifacts — agents define their own
searchIndex.body: gad-45 emergent agents.md stops mandating specific artifacts — agents define their own round 3 emergent agents.md prescribed architecture.md, changelog.md, specific directory layouts. that defeats the point of the emergent experiment, which is to observe what workflow artifacts an ai-decided workflow produces. v4 emergent agents.md only mandates: (1) put all workflow artifacts in game/.planning/, (2) reference the create_skill skill to define new skills when patterns emerge. everyth

searchIndex.title: "Traits" in UI, narrativeStats in code — split terminology
searchIndex.body: gad-44 "traits" in ui, narrativestats in code — split terminology player-facing ui shows narrative/progression stats as "traits". internal code/data can continue using narrativestats as the technical term. both must be documented with a mapping. prevents churn of a full taxonomy rename while giving players the more legible label.

searchIndex.title: Sprite sourcing is a workflow requirement with coherent fallback allowed
searchIndex.body: gad-43 sprite sourcing is a workflow requirement with coherent fallback allowed v4 requires agents to attempt asset sourcing first through a defined search workflow: lightweight installable icon packs, web-searched free/open-license sprites, or common art sources. fallback to generated/programmatic placeholders is allowed only if sourcing genuinely fails or integration cost becomes disproportionate — but the fallback must still be visually coherent. ugly primitive-only presen

searchIndex.title: Skills (physical actions) are a scored criterion, not a gate, in v4
searchIndex.body: gad-42 skills (physical actions) are a scored criterion, not a gate, in v4 skills system (physical non-spell actions that evolve via own xp, can be supercharged with mana crystals) was considered for gate status but deferred. forge is already a major gate and requiring both would cause broad-but-shallow implementations. v4 requires only that combat supports at least one non-spell action category (basic attack or skill). full skill system with evolution and mana-crystal o

searchIndex.title: v4 game requirements reframed: pressure over features
searchIndex.body: gad-41 v4 game requirements reframed: pressure over features round 3 findings exposed that v3 was a features checklist, not a game design. players could beat floors with default spells, forge was optional, no encounter tested adaptation. v4 flips this: every mechanic must create pressure that rewards ingenuity. core principle: "baseline starter abilities must not be sufficient to comfortably complete a full floor without adaptation or enhancement." specific

searchIndex.title: Eval projects categorized by mode (greenfield/brownfield) and workflow (gad/bare/emergent)
searchIndex.body: gad-40 eval projects categorized by mode (greenfield/brownfield) and workflow (gad/bare/emergent) evals are now categorized on two axes: eval_mode (greenfield starts from scratch, brownfield extends an existing baseline codebase) and workflow (gad uses full framework, bare creates own workflow, emergent inherits and evolves). stored in gad.json. shown in gad eval list. brownfield evals reference a baseline (project+version) whose run/ becomes the starting codebase. added three new brownfield e

searchIndex.title: All eval workflow artifacts must live under game/.planning/ regardless of framework
searchIndex.body: gad-39 all eval workflow artifacts must live under game/.planning/ regardless of framework gad, bare, and emergent evals all must place workflow/tracking/skills/decisions/notes files under game/.planning/. source code in src/, assets in public/, process artifacts in .planning/. this separation allows clean inspection of what methodology each eval invented without mixing it into source. the format inside .planning/ is up to the agent (xml, markdown, whatever). violations (files at projec

searchIndex.title: Eval preservation is a hard contract enforced by tests
searchIndex.body: gad-38 eval preservation is a hard contract enforced by tests every implementation eval run must end with these artifacts preserved at canonical locations: (1) evals/project/vn/trace.json, (2) evals/project/vn/run/ with code+planning, (3) apps/portfolio/public/evals/project/vn/ with the playable build, (4) evals/project/vn/.gad-log/ with cli logs. the gad eval preserve command copies everything after a run; gad eval verify audits compliance; tests/eval-prese

searchIndex.title: Eval game builds must be preserved per-version, never overwritten
searchIndex.body: gad-37 eval game builds must be preserved per-version, never overwritten previously, gad eval open copied builds to apps/portfolio/public/evals/project/index.html, overwriting previous runs. this destroyed experimental history. now all builds are preserved at apps/portfolio/public/evals/project/version/index.html. the gad eval open command checks version-specific paths first.

searchIndex.title: Freedom hypothesis: GAD constraint may correlate inversely with creative output quality
searchIndex.body: gad-36 freedom hypothesis: gad constraint may correlate inversely with creative output quality across 3 rounds of escape-the-dungeon evals, bare (no framework) consistently outperformed gad (full framework) on human review. round 3: bare v3 = 0.70, emergent v2 = 0.50, gad v8 = 0.20. gad has never exceeded 0.30 across 4 attempts; bare is monotonically improving (0.10 → 0.50 → 0.70). gad's planning overhead may consume tokens that could go to implementation and testing. the process is followe

searchIndex.title: Bare eval condition is a live contender, not a deprecated baseline
searchIndex.body: gad-35 bare eval condition is a live contender, not a deprecated baseline bare v2 scored 0.50 human review (most playable game) versus gad v7 at 0.30. the bare condition responds well when informed by prior failure signals, making it a valid forward-looking experimental condition. all three conditions (gad, bare, emergent) remain in active testing with equal priority.

searchIndex.title: Human review weight raised to 0.30 with low-score caps
searchIndex.body: gad-34 human review weight raised to 0.30 with low-score caps human_review weight increased from 0.15 to 0.30 in composite formula. added hard caps: if human_review < 0.20, composite capped at 0.40; if < 0.10, capped at 0.25. this prevents technically compliant but broken/unusable output from ranking highly. process metrics cannot override a bad user experience.

searchIndex.title: Spell crafting is a gate criterion — loot/rewards do not satisfy it
searchIndex.body: gad-33 spell crafting is a gate criterion — loot/rewards do not satisfy it the player must intentionally combine runes/components through a forge/crafting interface and produce at least one new spell outcome. pre-equipped spells or spell rewards from npcs/loot do not count. minimum: 3-5 runes, 3+ valid combinations, crafted spell usable in encounters.

searchIndex.title: UI quality is a gate criterion at full presentation level
searchIndex.body: gad-32 ui quality is a gate criterion at full presentation level the build must include icons, visually differentiated room presentation, styled controls/panels, and entity visual representation. easily installable icon/sprite packs, web-searched assets, or generated placeholders are acceptable when setup cost is low. raw ascii/text-only ui fails the gate. geometric fallback tiles are last resort, not primary path.

searchIndex.title: Inherited skills must capture failure modes and fixes, not just patterns
searchIndex.body: gad-31 inherited skills must capture failure modes and fixes, not just patterns emergent v1 inherited 4 skills from bare v1 but still failed (kaplay styled text error). the skills documented code patterns but not failure modes. bare v2 (told about v1 failure, no inherited skills) succeeded because it had to solve the problem itself. skills that only document "how i did it" without "what went wrong and how i fixed it" are insufficient for cross-run learning.

searchIndex.title: Eval requirements must be versioned and findings documented per experiment
searchIndex.body: gad-30 eval requirements must be versioned and findings documented per experiment requirements changed twice in one session (original 12 criteria → gate criteria + vertical slice). each change invalidates comparison with previous runs. requirements must carry a version tag, and findings docs must reference which requirement version was used. scientific rigor requires reproducibility.

searchIndex.title: Process metrics do not guarantee output quality — human review is the ground truth
searchIndex.body: gad-29 process metrics do not guarantee output quality — human review is the ground truth gad v7 scored 0.81 discipline and 1.0 planning quality but only 0.30 human review (broken game loop). bare v2 scored 0.0 discipline but 0.50 human review (most playable game). auto-composite can be misleading — the 15% human_review weight is insufficient. for implementation evals that produce user-facing output, human review should be weighted higher or serve as a gate.

searchIndex.title: Granular eval metrics: versioned schema, summary in TRACE, detail in optional metrics.json
searchIndex.body: gad-28 granular eval metrics: versioned schema, summary in trace, detail in optional metrics.json automation must not depend on regex-parsing human-oriented `score.md`. implementation evals gain `scores.json` (or equivalent) alongside `score.md` with stable numeric fields. tooling evals store a summary composite (`tooling_composite` or nested `scores`) in `trace.json` for gates and trends; high-cardinality data (per-invocation latencies, histogram buckets, full event traces) go in `metrics.jso

searchIndex.title: Tooling and MCP evals are automated, fixture-based, and comparable across versions
searchIndex.body: gad-27 tooling and mcp evals are automated, fixture-based, and comparable across versions evaluating watch/dev and mcp differs from implementation evals (agents building projects). tooling evals use `eval_type` of `tooling` or `mcp`, a fixed `scenario` id pointing at an in-repo fixture, and an automated harness (node script or `gad eval tooling run`) that starts the watcher or mcp client, performs scripted actions, and asserts ground truth (missing paths, exit codes) plus latency metri

searchIndex.title: Real-time GAD tooling: core first, transports second
searchIndex.body: gad-26 real-time gad tooling: core first, transports second the live developer loop (watch planning files, re-run refs verify, surface results quickly) is implemented once as a small core: debounced filesystem watch, call into existing verify code paths, emit structured events (json lines or ndjson) for consumers. cli (`gad dev` / `gad watch planning`) is the first transport. an mcp server, if added, is a thin adapter over the same core — not a separate im

searchIndex.title: All GAD CLI calls and agent tool uses are logged to JSONL for eval tracing
searchIndex.body: gad-25 all gad cli calls and agent tool uses are logged to jsonl for eval tracing every gad cli invocation logs to .planning/.gad-log/ (jsonl, one file per day): timestamp, command, args, duration, exit code. a posttooluse hook (tool-trace.js) logs every agent tool call (bash, read, write, edit, agent, skill, grep, glob) to the same directory. during eval runs, gad_log_dir routes logs to the eval run directory for permanent storage. `gad log show` displays both log types interl

searchIndex.title: All skills and agents must bootstrap via gad snapshot, not manual file reads
searchIndex.body: gad-24 all skills and agents must bootstrap via gad snapshot, not manual file reads skills were hardcoding "read state.md, roadmap.md, decisions.md, requirements.md" as step 1 — 4+ file reads that the gad snapshot command already provides in one low-token output. skills must use `gad snapshot --projectid <id>` as their bootstrap step. individual cli commands (gad tasks, gad decisions, gad phases) are available for drilling into detail. skills must also be format-agnostic (x

searchIndex.title: Greenfield eval projects must auto-scaffold CONVENTIONS.md after phase 01
searchIndex.body: gad-23 greenfield eval projects must auto-scaffold conventions.md after phase 01 the escape-the-dungeon agent created 13 source files with patterns (scene structure, content pack format, state management) but documented none of them. a follow-up agent would start cold with no conventions. after the first implementation phase, gad should prompt or auto-generate a conventions.md from the code patterns established — file structure, naming, import patterns, content pack format, sc

searchIndex.title: Trace logging must be automatic — hooks or checkpoint-based, not manual log-cmd calls
searchIndex.body: gad-22 trace logging must be automatic — hooks or checkpoint-based, not manual log-cmd calls manual trace logging (gad eval trace log-cmd/log-skill) requires the agent to remember to call them. the v2 eval proved this doesn't happen — the agent backfilled all 8 skill triggers in 15 seconds at the end. tracing should be: (1) automatic via hooks that detect gad cli calls and skill invocations, or (2) checkpoint-based where the agent must produce a trace entry to unlock the next phase, or (3

searchIndex.title: Eval agents must be bootstrapped with GAD context — not just prompted
searchIndex.body: gad-21 eval agents must be bootstrapped with gad context — not just prompted the escape-the-dungeon eval (v2) showed that prompting an agent to "use gad snapshot" and "log traces" doesn't work — the agent ignored the cli entirely, read requirements, and coded directly. eval agents need: (1) gad cli available and working in the worktree, (2) agents.md in the eval template that enforces the loop, (3) snapshot output injected into the agent's initial prompt (not "run this com

searchIndex.title: Snapshot includes project-scoped conventions with global fallback
searchIndex.body: gad-20 snapshot includes project-scoped conventions with global fallback gad snapshot includes a conventions section: first checks project-level conventions.md in .planning/, then extracts conventions/style sections from the project's agents.md, then falls back to global root agents.md conventions. conventions grow per-project as code/content/patterns evolve — refactoring introduces new conventions, and the snapshot surfaces them to agents. eval runs are expected to pr

searchIndex.title: GSD is dead — GAD and RepoPlanner only
searchIndex.body: gad-19 gsd is dead — gad and repoplanner only all gsd (get-shit-done) skills, hooks, commands, and references are removed from the repo. gad is the planning framework. repoplanner provides the planning snapshot format that gad consumes. no gsd commands in .claude/commands/, no gsd hooks in .claude/hooks/, no gsd references in settings.json or agents.md. the context monitor and statusline hooks are useful infrastructure but must be rebranded f

searchIndex.title: The GAD loop is simple: snapshot → work → update docs → commit
searchIndex.body:
gad-18 the gad loop is simple: snapshot → work → update docs → commit the canonical gad loop for any agent, any coding tool:
1. `gad snapshot --projectid <id>` — one command, full context. no reading 15 files manually.
2. pick one task from task-registry.xml (status=planned).
3. implement it.
4. update task-registry.xml (mark done), state.xml (next-action), decisions.xml (if new decisions).
5. commit.

that's it. agents.md files in each project referenc

searchIndex.title: Context exhaustion: work through auto-compact, do not stop
searchIndex.body: gad-17 context exhaustion: work through auto-compact, do not stop when context window fills, claude code auto-compacts. the agent works through it — stopping to start a fresh session is pointless friction. after compaction, run `gad snapshot --projectid <id>` or `gad context` to re-hydrate and continue. the planning docs are what make this possible — state.xml next-action and task-registry.xml tell the agent exactly where it was. no fresh sessions, no hand

searchIndex.title: GAD does not support subagents until they are formally evaluated
searchIndex.body: gad-16 gad does not support subagents until they are formally evaluated spawning fresh agent instances (subagents via the agent tool in claude code, or equivalent in other clis) is not part of the gad workflow. subagents start cold with no conversation history, burn context re-deriving state from files, introduce coordination overhead, and have uncharacterized failure modes. until a formal gad eval measures subagent utility against inline work, they are not a recommen

searchIndex.title: portfolio-bare eval uses real agent sessions, not a harness — context_mode is self-declared
searchIndex.body: gad-15 portfolio-bare eval uses real agent sessions, not a harness — context_mode is self-declared the portfolio-bare eval is a real agent session in a template directory (evals/portfolio-bare/template/) containing only planning docs and requirements — no app code. the agent opens the template in claude code or codex, runs `gad session new [--fresh]` to declare context mode, plans phases from requirements, executes tasks, and writes a trace at the end. no api keys, no execsync harness.

searchIndex.title: gad state and gad phases default to session project when a session is active
searchIndex.body: gad-14 gad state and gad phases default to session project when a session is active when no --projectid flag is given, `gad state`, `gad phases`, `gad decisions`, `gad requirements`, `gad errors`, `gad blockers`, and `gad refs` call getactivesessionprojectid() and scope output to the active session's project. explicit --projectid overrides. --all bypasses session scope entirely and shows all roots. gad ls always shows all roots unscoped.

searchIndex.title: TRACE.json is the machine-readable sidecar for every eval run
searchIndex.body: gad-13 trace.json is the machine-readable sidecar for every eval run every eval run directory must contain a trace.json alongside run.md and score.md. trace.json records: workflow (a/b/both), per-command token counts and unit fidelity, unit_coverage map (u1–u12 at full/referenced/truncated/approximated/absent), and computed totals. agents write it using `gad eval trace write` or inline during a run.

searchIndex.title: Missing-arg UX: print available choices and rerun hint, never a blind error
searchIndex.body: gad-12 missing-arg ux: print available choices and rerun hint, never a blind error all gad cli commands that require a --projectid or --id argument must, when absent, print the list of valid choices and a ready-to-run rerun hint on stderr before exiting 1. blind citty "missing required argument" errors are banned — required: true is replaced with our own check.

searchIndex.title: gad sink is the canonical docs sync command
searchIndex.body: gad-11 gad sink is the canonical docs sync command `gad sink` owns the four-direction docs sync surface: status (diff), compile (planning→docs), sync (compile all, non-destructive), decompile (ensure dirs, create stubs), validate (mapping health). sink is manual by default — no auto-compile on file change. human-authored sink files (no generated: true frontmatter) are never overwritten.

searchIndex.title: gad snapshot is the one-shot full project context command
searchIndex.body: gad-10 gad snapshot is the one-shot full project context command `gad snapshot` inlines every planning file in a project's .planning/ directory (excluding archive/, sessions/, node_modules/). priority order: state, roadmap, requirements, decisions, task-registry, then alphabetical. this is the canonical command for handing an agent a complete cold-start context pack. it differs from `gad context` — context gives session-aware minimal refs, snapshot gives everyt

searchIndex.title: gad context inlines file contents by default; --refs is the lightweight mode
searchIndex.body: gad-09 gad context inlines file contents by default; --refs is the lightweight mode the `gad context` command inlines all planning file contents directly into its output by default. this gives agents a single command to get full context without reading files separately. the `--refs` flag restores the lightweight behavior (file paths only). this improves the completeness score on the cli-efficiency eval.

searchIndex.title: GAD v1.0.0 defining characteristic: skills-first with evaluation
searchIndex.body: gad-08 gad v1.0.0 defining characteristic: skills-first with evaluation pre-1.0 (gsd/ralph-wiggum loop) was workflow-heavy — agents followed long workflow docs. v1.0.0 is skills-first: every skill has an agent-agnostic skill.md definition, is evaluated for output quality, and is versioned with the methodology. the cli provides context efficiency. evals are scientific. traces measure what agents retrieve. domain translators extend gad to verticals.

searchIndex.title: gad-manuscript is a self-contained domain translator repo
searchIndex.body: gad-07 gad-manuscript is a self-contained domain translator repo gad-manuscript is a standalone repo that defines the domain translator pattern — adapts gad base methodology to book/fiction creation. it owns its own skill.md files, evals, and planning docs while referencing gad:* base skills. the magicborn in-world lore content in the docs sink is produced by gad-manuscript skills.

searchIndex.title: GAD CLI is format-agnostic: XML, MD, MDX all valid in .planning/
searchIndex.body: gad-06 gad cli is format-agnostic: xml, md, mdx all valid in .planning/ sub-repos and .planning/ directories may use xml, md, or mdx. the gad cli must parse all three transparently. the planning sink normalizes to mdx only. compile() converts any format to mdx; decompile() restores the original format. coding agents and the cli treat methodology as the constant — format is an implementation detail.

searchIndex.title: repo-planner is officially a GAD project; rp-* skills are deprecated
searchIndex.body: gad-05 repo-planner is officially a gad project; rp-* skills are deprecated vendor/repo-planner is registered as a gad root (id=repo-planner) in planning-config.toml. it has a gad-format .planning/ directory. its skills (rp-*) are deprecated stubs pointing to gad:* equivalents. the manuscript skill content migrated to vendor/get-anything-done/skills/manuscript/skill.md. the portfolio docs remain as the docs sink.

searchIndex.title: All session intents, decisions, and guidance must be captured in planning docs each session
searchIndex.body: gad-04 all session intents, decisions, and guidance must be captured in planning docs each session every working session that produces new decisions, requirements, or guidance must update decisions.xml and the relevant planning docs before the session ends. chat context is ephemeral — planning docs are the durable record. the gad planning loop is the mechanism; this decision makes the capture requirement explicit.

searchIndex.title: Context = session-variable information units; static docs are exempt from CLI coverage
searchIndex.body: gad-03 context = session-variable information units; static docs are exempt from cli coverage context for agent resumption is defined as the 10 session-variable information units (u1–u10) in definitions.md. static docs (agents.md style guide, planning model reference tables, done-task history) do not constitute context loss if absent from cli output — they are read once and cached. context loss occurs only when a unit's absence would cause an agent to make a different next-action decision.

searchIndex.title: Evals capture actual content, not just byte counts — token counts are the primary efficiency metric
searchIndex.body: gad-02 evals capture actual content, not just byte counts — token counts are the primary efficiency metric every eval run.md must contain verbatim cli and raw workflow outputs, a unit-by-unit fidelity table, token counts (chars/4), arithmetic showing all formulas applied, and an agent simulation proving the next-action decision is identical with and without the cli. byte counts alone are insufficient because they cannot reveal whether information is actually readable or complete.

searchIndex.title: GAD is the universal planning framework — agent-agnostic, not Claude Code-specific
searchIndex.body: gad-01 gad is the universal planning framework — agent-agnostic, not claude code-specific gad (get-anything-done) is the single planning framework for all projects in this repo. skills are methodology documents (skill.md) stored in vendor/get-anything-done/skills/ — they must work with any ai coding assistant, not just claude code. claude code-specific install paths (~/.claude/) are implementation details, not the canonical source.

searchIndex.title: Create .planning/ scaffold for get-anything-done and register in planning-config.toml.
searchIndex.body: 10-01 create .planning/ scaffold for get-anything-done and register in planning-config.toml. planning scaffold registration

searchIndex.title: Migrate gad-01 through gad-15 decisions from root DECISIONS.xml into vendor/get-anything-done/.planning/DECISIONS.xml. A
searchIndex.body: 10-02 migrate gad-01 through gad-15 decisions from root decisions.xml into vendor/get-anything-done/.planning/decisions.xml. add gad-16 (no subagents until evaluated) and gad-17 (context handoff via planning docs). decisions migration subagents context-handoff

searchIndex.title: Add gad pack command that bundles .planning data into a repo-planner-compatible planning pack format (JSON bundle with s
searchIndex.body: 10-03 add gad pack command that bundles .planning data into a repo-planner-compatible planning pack format (json bundle with state, phases, tasks, decisions, requirements, docrefs). pack planning-pack repo-planner

searchIndex.title: Create vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md using GAD's own workflow — gad CLI commands, planning loop, context re-hydrati
searchIndex.body: 10-04 create vendor/get-anything-done/agents.md using gad's own workflow — gad cli commands, planning loop, context re-hydration, skill references. agents documentation workflow

searchIndex.body: 22-50 extract thinking blocks from session.jsonl into trace.json derived.reasoning_trace (gad-84, gaps g13). post-hoc offline enrichment — no new hooks required. walk a run's session.jsonl, pull every thinking block and inter-tool message text, attach to trace.json with timestamps tying thoughts to subsequent tool uses. first exploratory analysis: thought-to-action interval, thought length distribution, keyword presence (exploration vs execution), thought-before-action vs thought-after-action ordering. this is the highest-leverage way to get "agent reasoning" signal without claude code exposing new hook surfaces. thinking-blocks session-jsonl reasoning-trace post-hoc

searchIndex.title: Phase 14 — Eval framework: escape-the-dungeon + tracing
